items.title: A hacker ran me over with a robot lawn mower
items.summary: I'm lying in the dirt. It's coming for me. Then, with a lurch, it's climbing up my chest. If Andreas Makris doesn't stop the 200-pound robot lawn mower in time, it could drag its blades across my body. Makris certainly can't reach over and hit the emergency stop button - he's nearly 6,000 miles away, […]

items.title: Just $6.50 gets you this 100W 3.3-foot USB-C cable that neatly retracts into its shell when not in use (50% off)
items.summary:
Baseus 100W 3.3-foot Retractable USB-C Cable in black

Buy for $6.50 (Reg. $13)

Apply 5K7Y7M7U during checkout.

What we love

I’ve gradually swapped out most of my regular USB-C cables for retractable ones like these whereever possible, mainly because they’re far easier to store and carry around. You don’t have to deal with tangled cords or excess cable cluttering up your desk, backpack, or car, and the retractable mechanism itself is quick and convenient to use. The model featured here includes a 3.3-foot cable with support for up to 100W power delivery.

The former of these two is taking the mantle of Govee’s latest flagship lighting device, with color reproduction powered by LuminBlend+ color management tech, while the former is bringing along a “first-of-its-kind” Halo Gradient lighting experience to a more lantern-focused form factor. items.title: Huge $1,000 discount strikes Lenovo’s fully-loaded RTX 5090 Legion Pro 7i OLED gaming laptop today (All-time low)
items.summary:
We’re now tracking a deal at B&H that drops Lenovo’s Legion Pro 7i OLED gaming laptop with an RTX 5090 GPU down to $2,999 shipped. While the listing carries a $3,999 sticker price at B&H, configuring the same machine with a Core Ultra 275HX processor, 32GB of RAM, and a 2TB SSD currently comes out to $4,073.27, meaning today’s offer saves you more than $1,074. We’ve previously seen discounts on other Legion Pro 7i configurations featuring RTX 5070 Ti and RTX 5080 graphics, along with even steeper specs like the 64GB RAM model, but this marks the first time this particular RTX 5090 variant has dropped by over $1,000. It’s also a particularly compelling deal in the broader RTX 5090 laptop market, where similarly-equipped machines – and even many RTX 5080 models – still tend to sell for well above the $3,000 mark at Best Buy and other retailers. Head below for more details.

more…
